title: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiels now famous screams
display_title: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiels now famous screams
wikitext:
[]: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiel's now-famous screams?             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Mar 16, 2023 10:00 am Random question I know but it just popped into my head the other day. Did Khara get someone in to bust their lungs or did they just use stock audio?

[]: Re: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiel's now-famous screams?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 17, 2023 11:23 am That scream was also used in Diebuster in 2004, IIRC, about 3 years before Eva 1.x’s use of it in its final Angel battle. Edit: While not the same as the angel’s death scream, this seems to be related to another one of their screams.

[]: Re: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiel's now-famous screams?             Quote Post by 2Lacissal2 » Fri Mar 17, 2023 2:21 pm Semi related but Ramiel’s famous choral ‘haaaaa’ noise had been used before in the original Gundam (1979) for Lalah Sune’s Elmeth, but watching through Ultraseven (1967) recently the same sound effect was used for a spaceship (I’ll have to look through some episode descriptions and find the exact episode) and I’d bet it’d been used before that in some older toku movie.

[]: Re: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiel's now-famous screams?             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Mar 17, 2023 4:37 pm Yeah, a lot of sound effects in scifi are, like physical props, used for a lot of things and go way further back than you'd think at first

[]: Re: Do we know who did Rebuild Ramiel's now-famous screams?             Quote Post by 2Lacissal2 » Wed Mar 29, 2023 8:11 am Fittingly I just watched Atragon (1963) and am 98% certain Ramiel's choral voice sound effect is clipped from the opening vocal note of the OST piece 'Prayer of the Mu Empire' https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fJ2hE05VC-8
